evals.prompt: Our Next.js app is growing fast — we have 30+ features all mixed in a components/ folder. How should we restructure?
evals.expected_output: Adopt Feature-Sliced Design (FSD): create src/features/[feature]/ with ui/, model/, api/ segments. Layer hierarchy: App -> Widgets -> Features -> Entities -> Shared. Group by business domain (features/auth/login/) not file type (components/, hooks/). Each slice exports via index.ts public API.

evals.prompt: I have useEffect and fetch calls directly inside my page.tsx files.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No business logic in page.tsx. Pages import Widgets/Features only — zero useEffect/fetch in page files. Move fetch to Server Components in features/*/api/. Move useEffect logic to custom hooks in features/*/model/. Pages should be thin wrappers that compose widgets.

evals.prompt: Two features in my app directly import from each other — the cart feature imports from the product feature and vice versa.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No cross-slice imports. Slices in the same layer must not import from each other. Fix: extract shared data/types to a lower layer (Entities or Shared). Create entities/product/ for shared product types. Both cart and product features import from entities/product/ — never from each other.

should_not_trigger: Configure ESLint rules for the project
Write unit tests for the order service
Set up Docker Compose for development
